description: As a TUI developer, I want a Section interface so that I can create list-based interactive views distinct from wizard Fields
steps: Create pkg/tui/section.go with Section interface
Interface includes Init, Update, View methods (Bubble Tea Model)
Update method returns (Section, tea.Cmd) tuple
Interface includes Focus, Blur lifecycle methods returning tea.Cmd
Interface includes WithTheme, WithWidth, WithHeight configuration methods
Define SectionCompleteMsg struct with Result any field
Define SectionCancelMsg struct for cancel/quit signaling
Verify: Code compiles with no errors

description: As a TUI developer, I want extended GitService interface so that worktree list can fetch worktree and status data
steps: Add ListWorktrees() ([]Worktree, error) method to GitService interface
Add GetBranchStatus(worktreePath string) (*BranchStatus, error) method
Add PullWorktree(worktreePath string) error method
Add PushWorktree(worktreePath string) error method
Add RemoveWorktree(name string) error method
Add GetCurrentWorktree() (*Worktree, error) method
Define Worktree struct with Name, Branch, Path, IsBare fields
Define BranchStatus struct with Ahead, Behind, UpToDate, NoRemote fields
Verify: Code compiles with no errors

description: As a TUI developer, I want a reusable table component so that I can display worktrees in a navigable list
steps: Create pkg/tui/components/table/table.go
Define Column struct with Title, Width, MinWidth, MaxWidth, Grow, Priority fields
Define Row struct with Cells []string and Data any fields
Create Model struct wrapping bubbles/table with theme support
Implement New(columns, theme) constructor function
Implement SetRows(rows []Row) method
Implement SelectedRow() *Row method returning current selection
Implement Cursor() int method returning current position
Implement Init, Update, View Bubble Tea methods
Implement Focus, Blur methods for lifecycle management
Verify: Table displays rows and allows navigation with arrow keys

description: As a user, I want the table columns to adjust to terminal width so that I can see relevant information at any size
steps: Implement SetWidth(width int) method that triggers column recalculation
Implement SetHeight(height int) method for visible rows
Implement computeColumnWidths() private method
Algorithm: Start with base widths, hide low-priority columns if too wide
Algorithm: Distribute extra space to Grow columns proportionally
Algorithm: Respect MinWidth and MaxWidth constraints
Handle WindowSizeMsg to update dimensions
Verify: Resize terminal and see columns adjust appropriately

description: As a user, I want an inline confirmation prompt so that I can confirm destructive actions like delete
steps: Create pkg/tui/components/prompt/prompt.go
Define ConfirmMsg struct for confirm signal
Define CancelMsg struct for cancel signal
Create Model struct with visible, question, selected, theme fields
Implement New(theme) constructor (hidden by default)
Implement Show(question string) method
Implement Hide() method
Implement IsVisible() bool method
Handle y/Y key for immediate confirm (returns ConfirmMsg)
Handle n/N/esc key for immediate cancel (returns CancelMsg)
Handle left/right/tab to toggle Yes/No selection
Handle enter to confirm current selection
Implement View() rendering: 'Question? [Yes] No' format
Verify: Prompt appears, responds to keys, and sends correct messages

description: As a user, I want a footer with help text and messages so that I know available actions and see feedback
steps: Create pkg/tui/components/footer/footer.go
Define MessageStyle enum: MessageInfo, MessageSuccess, MessageError
Create Model struct with helpText, message, msgStyle, theme, width fields
Implement New(helpText, theme) constructor
Implement SetMessage(msg, style) method for transient messages
Implement ClearMessage() method
Implement SetWidth(width) method
Implement View() rendering message (if present) plus help text
Style messages according to MessageStyle (success=green, error=red)
Verify: Footer displays help text and styled messages

description: As a TUI developer, I want WorktreeRow data type so that I can represent worktree state for display
steps: Create pkg/tui/sections/worktrees/row.go
Define WorktreeRow struct with Name, Branch, Path, Kind, IsCurrent fields
Add Status *BranchStatus field for sync status
Add Loading bool and Error error fields for async state
Implement ToTableRow() method returning table.Row
Mark current worktree with '* ' prefix in Name cell
Implement formatStatus() helper for status symbols
Show '⋯' for loading, '✗' for error, '—' for N/A
Show '✓' for up-to-date, '?' for no remote
Show '↑ N' for ahead, '↓ N' for behind, '↕ N↑M↓' for diverged
Verify: WorktreeRow converts to displayable table row correctly

description: As a TUI developer, I want async status loading so that status fetches don't block the UI
steps: Create pkg/tui/sections/worktrees/status.go
Define StatusLoadedMsg struct with Name, Status, Error fields
Create StatusLoader struct with evaluators map and git service
Implement NewStatusLoader(git) constructor
Implement LoadStatus(name, path) tea.Cmd for single worktree
Implement LoadAll(worktrees) tea.Cmd for concurrent loading
Implement Refresh(name, path) tea.Cmd for reload after action
Implement IsLoading(name) bool method
Use async.Eval for caching and concurrency
Verify: Status loads asynchronously and sends StatusLoadedMsg

description: As a TUI developer, I want WorktreeSection model so that it orchestrates table, prompt, and footer
steps: Create pkg/tui/sections/worktrees/section.go
Create Model struct with table, prompt, footer components
Add worktrees []WorktreeRow and statusLoader *StatusLoader fields
Add focused, selectedPath, ctx, theme, width, height fields
Implement New(ctx) constructor creating all components
Implement SelectedPath() and SelectedName() accessor methods
Implement tui.Section interface methods
Verify: Section creates and holds all child components

description: As a user, I want the worktree list to load on startup so that I see my worktrees immediately
steps: Implement Init() that calls ListWorktrees from GitService
Convert Worktree list to WorktreeRow list
Set rows on table component
Start async status loading for all worktrees via statusLoader.LoadAll()
Handle errors from ListWorktrees gracefully
Verify: On init, table populates with worktrees and status starts loading

description: As a user, I want to navigate worktrees with keyboard so that I can browse and select
steps: Handle up/down arrow keys for table navigation
Handle j/k vim keys for table navigation
Handle enter/space to select worktree and emit SectionCompleteMsg
Set selectedPath to chosen worktree's path on selection
Handle q/esc to quit and emit SectionCancelMsg
Handle ctrl+c for force quit
Delegate unhandled keys to table component
Verify: Navigate with arrows/vim keys, select with enter, quit with q/esc

description: As a user, I want to pull a worktree with 'l' key so that I can sync with remote
steps: Handle 'l' key to trigger pull action
Get selected worktree from table
Show loading state in status column
Call GitService.PullWorktree(path) in command
Define PullCompleteMsg with Name and Error fields
Handle PullCompleteMsg: show success/error in footer
Refresh status for pulled worktree
Verify: Press 'l', see loading, then updated status

description: As a user, I want to push a worktree with 'p' key so that I can send commits to remote
steps: Handle 'p' key to trigger push action
Check if branch is tracked (via trackedBranches map)
If tracked, show error message in footer and do nothing
If not tracked, show loading state and call GitService.PushWorktree(path)
Define PushCompleteMsg with Name and Error fields
Handle PushCompleteMsg: show success/error in footer
Refresh status for pushed worktree
Verify: Press 'p' on tracked branch shows error, on untracked succeeds

description: As a user, I want to delete a worktree with 'd' key so that I can clean up unused worktrees
steps: Handle 'd' key to show delete confirmation prompt
Get selected worktree and compose question string
Call prompt.Show(question) to display confirmation
While prompt visible, delegate all input to prompt.Update()
Handle prompt.ConfirmMsg: call GitService.RemoveWorktree(name)
Handle prompt.CancelMsg: hide prompt and return to table
Define DeleteCompleteMsg with Name and Error fields
Handle DeleteCompleteMsg: remove row from table, show message
Verify: Press 'd', confirm with y, worktree removed from list

description: As a user, I want to see async status updates so that I know sync state without waiting
steps: Handle StatusLoadedMsg in Update()
Find matching WorktreeRow by Name
Update Status, Loading, Error fields from message
Regenerate table row with ToTableRow()
Update table via SetRows()
Verify: Status column shows spinner then resolves to symbol

description: As a user, I want the section to adapt to terminal resize so that layout remains usable
steps: Handle tea.WindowSizeMsg in Update()
Update m.width and m.height from message
Propagate width to table via SetWidth()
Calculate table height (total height minus footer height)
Propagate height to table via SetHeight()
Propagate width to footer via SetWidth()
Verify: Resize terminal, table and footer adjust correctly

description: As a user, I want a clean rendered view so that I can see worktrees clearly
steps: Implement View() method composing table + footer
If prompt visible, render prompt between table and footer
Use lipgloss.JoinVertical for layout
Ensure consistent width across components
Verify: View renders table, optional prompt, and footer

description: As a TUI developer, I want mock GitService with worktree data so that I can test without real repos
steps: Add worktrees []tui.Worktree field to MockGitService
Add branchStatuses map[string]*tui.BranchStatus field
Add trackedBranches map[string]bool field
Add currentWorktree string field
Implement WithWorktrees([]Worktree) builder method
Implement WithBranchStatuses(map) builder method
Implement WithTrackedBranches([]string) builder method
Implement WithCurrentWorktree(name) builder method
Verify: Mock can be configured with worktree test data

description: As a TUI developer, I want mock GitService operations so that I can test actions
steps: Implement ListWorktrees() returning mock worktrees with delay
Implement GetBranchStatus(path) looking up status by path→branch
Implement PullWorktree(path) simulating pull with delay
Implement PushWorktree(path) simulating push with delay
Implement RemoveWorktree(name) removing from mock list
Implement GetCurrentWorktree() returning mock current worktree
Implement IsTrackedBranch(branch) checking trackedBranches map
Verify: All mock operations work with configured data

description: As a developer, I want testls command so that I can test the worktree list TUI
steps: Create cmd/service/worktree_testls.go
Implement newWorktreeTestlsCommand(svc) returning *cobra.Command
Add --delay int flag (0-5000ms range, default 0)
Validate delay range and return error if invalid
Create MockGitService with sample worktrees (6 entries)
Configure mock with various branch statuses (up-to-date, ahead, behind, diverged, no remote)
Configure tracked branches (main, release/*)
Set current worktree to one of the samples
Verify: Command parses flags and creates configured mock

description: As a developer, I want testls to launch the TUI so that I can interact with mock worktrees
steps: Create tui.Context with mock service and dimensions
Create worktrees.New(ctx) section
Open /dev/tty for TUI input
Create tea.NewProgram with section and tea.WithAltScreen()
Run program and capture final model
If section completed (has SelectedPath), print path to stdout
Print success message to stderr
If cancelled, exit cleanly
Verify: Run 'gbm wt testls', TUI appears with mock worktrees

description: As a developer, I want --delay flag so that I can test loading states
steps: Pass delay to MockGitService.WithDelay()
Delay applies to ListWorktrees and GetBranchStatus calls
Status shows loading spinner during delay
Verify: Run 'gbm wt testls --delay 500', see spinners during load

description: As a developer, I want testls registered under 'wt' command so that I can invoke it
steps: Register testls subcommand in worktree.go
Command available as 'gbm wt testls'
Help text describes testing purpose
Verify: 'gbm wt testls --help' shows usage

description: As a user, I want full navigation to work so that I can browse worktrees
steps: Launch testls, see table with 6 worktrees
Press down arrow, cursor moves to next row
Press up arrow, cursor moves to previous row
Press j, cursor moves down
Press k, cursor moves up
Cursor wraps at boundaries
Verify: All navigation keys work correctly

description: As a user, I want selection to output path so that shell integration works
steps: Launch testls, navigate to a worktree
Press enter to select
TUI exits cleanly
Selected worktree path printed to stdout
Success message printed to stderr
Verify: path=$(gbm wt testls) captures correct path

description: As a user, I want quit to exit cleanly so that I can cancel without selection
steps: Launch testls, press q
TUI exits without outputting path
No error message shown
Press esc, same behavior
Press ctrl+c, same behavior
Verify: All quit methods exit cleanly

description: As a user, I want pull/push/delete actions so that I can manage worktrees
steps: Launch testls with --delay 200
Press 'l' to pull, see loading state, then success message
Navigate to tracked branch, press 'p', see error message
Navigate to non-tracked branch, press 'p', see success
Press 'd' to delete, see confirmation prompt
Press 'n' to cancel, prompt hides
Press 'd' again, press 'y' to confirm, row removed
Verify: All actions work with appropriate feedback

description: As a user, I want resize to work so that I can use any terminal size
steps: Launch testls in wide terminal (120+ chars)
All columns visible with expanded widths
Resize to narrow terminal (80 chars)
Low-priority columns hidden automatically
Resize to very narrow (60 chars)
Only essential columns remain
Verify: Table adapts to terminal width changes

description: As a user, I want to see status symbols so that I know sync state
steps: Launch testls, see status column
Up-to-date worktree shows '✓'
Ahead worktree shows '↑ N'
Behind worktree shows '↓ N'
Diverged worktree shows '↕ N↑M↓'
No remote worktree shows '?'
During load, shows '⋯'
Verify: All status symbols display correctly
